title: New Economic Data Highlights the Strength and Resilience of President Trump’s Economy
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Recent economic data shows strong growth and resilience in the U.S. economy under President Trump, with notable progress in housing and manufacturing. GDP growt
excerpt: Recent economic data shows strong growth and resilience in the U.S. economy under President Trump, with notable progress in housing and manufacturing. GDP growth has exceeded expectations, and job creation is robust. This indicates a thriving economy prioritizing American jobs and manufacturing.

content.introduction: Recent economic indicators paint a compelling picture of robust growth and resilience in the U.S. economy, with significant improvements in housing and manufacturing sectors. The data reveals a trajectory of expansion that has exceeded many analysts' expectations, suggesting strong underlying economic fundamentals.
body:
## GDP Growth and Economic Expansion

The U.S. economy has demonstrated remarkable performance with GDP growth consistently outpacing forecasts in recent quarters. The latest figures show a quarterly expansion rate of 3.2%, significantly higher than the 2.1% average recorded during the previous administration. This acceleration in economic output reflects increased consumer spending, business investment, and export activity. Manufacturing output has risen by 4.5% year-over-year, marking the strongest growth rate in nearly five years. The Institute for Supply Management's manufacturing index has remained above the 50-point expansion threshold for 18 consecutive months, indicating sustained sector expansion. These figures collectively suggest a broad-based economic recovery with momentum across multiple industries.

## Labor Market Strength

The employment landscape continues to show remarkable strength with unemployment rates falling to a 50-year low of 3.5%. The economy has added approximately 250,000 jobs per month on average over the past year, with particularly strong gains in manufacturing and construction sectors. Wage growth has accelerated to 3.4% annually, outpacing inflation and indicating improving purchasing power for workers. The labor force participation rate has increased to 63.4%, reflecting growing confidence among potential workers about available opportunities. This robust job creation has not only reduced unemployment but has also drawn discouraged workers back into the labor market, contributing positively to long-term economic growth prospects.

## Housing Market Recovery

The housing sector has shown significant recovery with new home construction rising by 12.3% year-over-year, reaching levels not seen since 2007. The National Association of Home Builders/Wells Fargo Housing Market Index has remained above the 50-point threshold indicating market expansion for 14 consecutive months. Existing home sales have increased by 5.1%, while home prices have appreciated at a moderate pace of 4.2%, suggesting a healthier balance between supply and demand. Mortgage rates have stabilized around 3.8%, making homeownership more accessible to a broader range of buyers. This revitalization in the housing sector has stimulated related industries such as construction, manufacturing of building materials, and home furnishings, creating a positive multiplier effect throughout the economy.

## Manufacturing Renaissance

The manufacturing sector has experienced a notable renaissance with industrial production increasing by 3.8% over the past year. The durable goods orders have shown consistent growth, particularly in capital goods excluding aircraft, which rose by 6.2%. The manufacturing PMI has remained above the expansion threshold, with new orders and production components showing particularly strong performance. Foreign direct investment in U.S. manufacturing has increased by 23%, with companies citing improved business conditions, regulatory environment, and skilled workforce availability. This resurgence in manufacturing has been accompanied by increased domestic production of critical goods, reducing reliance on foreign supply chains and enhancing national economic security.

content.outlook: Looking ahead, The economic policies of the first Trump Administration proved suc­ cessful, yielding higher economic growth and lower unemployment rates than projected by the Congressional Budget Office (CBO) in January 2017 and by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) in December 2016, as displayed in figures . Market participants will continue to monitor developments closely for signals about the direction of economic indicators trends and their broader implications for portfolio allocation and risk management strategies.
